name: two distinct people named Zane silently collapse to one entity
description: Adversarial M3/bare-first-name probe. Two genuinely different people share the first name 'Zane' (a sister and a coworker) and the model emits the bare name 'Zane' in both notes. docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Bare first names [decided: auto-link + retro-recheck]' says the moment a SECOND entity with the same name appears, prior auto-linked mentions are flagged for retroactive re-review. But entities.resolve_entity is layer-1 exact-alias only: note 2's 'Zane' finds exactly ONE existing entity and auto-links to it, so a second entity is never created, no ambiguous_mention is filed, and the coworker's 'occupation' fact is silently attached to the SISTER entity. No retro-recheck exists. This pins the real (wrong) behaviour: a single Zane entity carries BOTH facts and there is NO review item — the cross-person conflation is invisible. XFAIL: the spec promises a retro-review flag the pipeline never raises. Failing line: "expected a review item matching {'kind': 'ambiguous_mention'}, found none".
xfail: Bare-first-name retro-recheck still unreachable: the spec's auto-link rule fires (exactly one exact 'Zane' match, entities.resolve_entity layer 1) and extraction emits the identical bare name, so the layered resolver (relationship hop / embedding / entity.disambiguate) never even runs and a second Zane entity is never minted — leaving the retro-recheck, which triggers on second-entity creation, with nothing to fire on. Splitting these requires co-mention-signal disambiguation of exact-match bare names (note context vs the candidate's facts), a deliberate non-goal of layers 2-3 (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Bare first names').

steps.body: My sister Zane just adopted a rescue greyhound.
Zane from the data team got promoted to staff engineer.

facts.statement: Zane is a staff engineer on the data team.
